steps.title: Recognize the defining statement
steps.content: The relative clause defines what the Purkinje effect is—a phenomenon describing the eye's color perception in low light. When defining what something is or what it does, use simple present tense.

steps.title: Match the tense to the definition
steps.content: Simple present “is” (D) is the standard tense for defining what phenomena are and do. The other options fail: “has been” (ongoing process), “was” (past only), “will have been” (future)—all fail to express a timeless definition. That matches choice D — D is correct.
